sections.title: B.4 — Component Category Map: Flow Sensor
sections.content:
| # | BOM Path | Function | Safety-Critical | Alternatives at position |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | OT-1/2/3/4 › L4.3 › L3.7 › L2.6 Flow Meter Assembly | Confirm minimum flow for burner interlock; protect HX from dry-fire | **YES** (HX destruction risk on dry-fire) | Impeller 40% / Ultrasonic 30% / Mag 15% / dP 8% / Thermal 5% / Vortex 2% |
| 2 | OT-6 › L4.3 › L3.7 › L2.6 | Verify minimum loop flow for radiant manifold zones | NO (trim / energy metering) | Same pool; impeller-heavy |
| 3 | OT-7 › L4.3 › L3.7 › L2.6 | Measure pool water flow for safety and energy billing | YES (freeze / bypass protection) | Same pool |

**Insight:** Marquardt DN20 (transit-time, single-body flow + temperature) targets position 1 across all fuel-type boilers (OT-1–OT-4). Safety-criticality is highest in OT-1 condensing boilers, where dry-fire destroys the Al-Si or SS HX. Ultrasonic wins vs. mechanical impeller because it (a) has no moving parts to foul in glycol/inhibitor water, (b) covers the full 4.2–46.6 l/min range in a single DN20 body, and (c) outputs both flow rate and temperature — enabling single-device BTU calculation. The 30% current share growing against the 40% impeller base defines the primary displacement opportunity.

---

---

# Per-Output-Type BOM Trees

> Each tree is self-contained and complete from L4 subsystem to L0 raw material. Alternatives and market share appear at every level where a design choice exists. Output types with different combustion or construction principles have materially different trees.

---

sections.title: OUTPUT TYPE OT-2: Gas Non-Condensing Boiler
sections.content:
*Cast-iron sectional or steel fire-tube atmospheric boiler. No condensate recovery; flue temperature 150–250 °C. Simpler BOM than OT-1 — no condensate system, atmospheric draft, less electronics.*

```

│
├── L4.1  Heat Generation
│   │
```
